title: Lab 32: SSH Key Authentication
description: Configure passwordless SSH login from client to server using a key pair

tasks: On client, create user relay32 and set the password to cinder9.
On server, create user vault32 and set the password to cinder9.
On client, configure key-based SSH authentication so that user relay32 on client can log in to vault32@server without a password prompt.
hints: Generate the key as the source user.
Populate authorized_keys for the target user.
checks: getent passwd relay32 >/dev/null && getent shadow relay32 | awk -F: 'length($2)>0 && $2 !~ /^(!!?|\*|LK|NP)$/ {ok=1} END {exit !ok}'
# server getent passwd vault32 >/dev/null && getent shadow vault32 | awk -F: 'length($2)>0 && $2 !~ /^(!!?|\*|LK|NP)$/ {ok=1} END {exit !ok}'
runuser -l relay32 -c 'ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o BatchMode=yes vault32@server true'
